title: New Hampshire man arrested in 1993 homicide of Maine woman
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Daniel Jolly, 59, was arrested in New Hampshire for the alleged 1993 murder of Maxine Bitomski in Kittery, Maine.
Jolly, linked to Bitomski through his job at Medical Market, will be extradited to Maine for prosecution.
Maine State Police and Kittery Police have been investigating the case since 1993, with renewed focus in 2021 leading to the arrest.

title: Red Sox demote Brayan Bello after another disastrous start
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Brayan Bello was demoted to Triple-A Worcester after an 8-2 loss to the Orioles.
Bello struggled this season with a 16.88 ERA in the first inning and a 9.68 ERA in starting roles.
Despite a strong 2025 season, Bello's 2026 performance forced the Red Sox to re-evaluate his role.

title: Mass. police department decries dangerous behavior on e-bikes after 3 youth charged
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: Since November 2024, Attleboro police received 143 reports of teens operating e-bikes in reckless manners causing safety concerns.
A 16-year-old was charged with several offenses after a dangerous e-bike incident involving an Attleboro police officer.
Two teens were arrested for attempting to retrieve an e-bike from police storage after it was seized as evidence.

title: Keep a look out for the Northern Lights over New England skies Thursday into Friday
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: The aurora borealis may be visible over New England from Thursday night into Friday morning, says NOAA.
Peak viewing for the northern lights will be between 10 p.m. and 2 a.m., avoid light-polluted areas.
Camera sensors are better at capturing aurora colors, so use smartphones for enhanced viewing.

title: Am I required to take my new vehicle to the dealer for maintenance?
author: John Paul
bullets: Car owners are not required to service new vehicles at the dealer to maintain warranty protection.
Using a trusted mechanic is acceptable if the correct parts and oil specifications are followed.
Keep detailed records of maintenance performed, including date, mileage, and materials used.

title: Worcester man gets 4 to 7 years in prison for killing neighbor; family calls sentence a ‘slap in the face’
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Enrique Canales-Martinez, 27, received a 4 to 7-year sentence for manslaughter in the death of his neighbor.
Jason Mayhew, 36, was killed following an argument over loud music at their Worcester apartment building.
Mayhew's family criticized the sentence, calling it a 'slap in the face' compared to the recommended 14 to 16 years.

title: Holyoke church cancels service, temporarily closes after theft
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: St. Paul’s Episcopal Church in Holyoke canceled services after a break-in between May 24 and May 27.
Thieves stole food, valuables, and raffle prizes for the church's annual fundraiser supporting renovations and a daycare.
The church changed locks and is implementing security measures, while police investigate the theft.

title: NFL insider: ‘Good chance’ Patriots will sign Christian Gonzalez to huge contract extension
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Patriots likely to offer Christian Gonzalez a large contract extension before the season starts, says NFL's Ian Rapoport.
Gonzalez, a Pro Bowl cornerback, is set to earn $2.2 million this year; salary will rise under fifth-year option in 2027.
Coach Mike Vrabel expects Gonzalez to attend next week's mandatory minicamp despite his absence from OTAs.

title: Police investigating suspected arson at Boston’s Museum of African American History
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Boston Police investigate suspected arson at the Museum of African American History involving a burned package with Juneteenth-themed puzzles.
The incident is being treated as a suspected hate crime with alleged anti-Black racial bias, says the Boston Police Department.
The museum's president, Noelle Trent, emphasizes the ongoing challenges in preserving Black history amid increasing societal opposition.

title: Woman, 69, killed in Swampscott fire
author: Carson Lyle
bullets: A 69-year-old woman died in a fire at her Swampscott apartment on Thursday, according to the district attorney's office.
The fire occurred in a condominium complex on Loring Avenue. The woman was found in her apartment and pronounced dead at a hospital.
The cause of the fire is under investigation by State Police, Swampscott authorities, and the State Fire Marshal's office.
